title: Council Housebuilding Skills and Capacity Programme‚Äč     ‚Äč
body: "‚ÄčThe Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government has funded and launched the Council Housebuilding Skills and Capacity Programme (CHSCP).¬†¬†¬† ¬†  ‚ÄčCHSCP aims to support councils to increase their levels of housebuilding. It will be delivered in partnership with Homes England and the Local Government Association (LGA), with a mandate to upskill councils‚Äô existing workforces, recruit and retain graduate talent to specialist positions, and drive increased engagement with the government‚Äôs new ¬£39 billion, 10-year Social and Affordable Homes Programme (SAHP).¬†¬† ¬†  ‚ÄčThis follows the establishment of the Association of Directors of Housing in March 2025, in partnership with the LGA, which aims to be the leading professional voice for councils in England on housing and homelessness, bridging the gap between local expertise and national policy. This includes acting as a focal point for the sector to collaborate and share best practice relating to the delivery of social and affordable housing.¬†  ‚ÄčCHSCP has been developed following engagement with a range of stakeholders to assess the needs and capacity of both councils with housing revenue accounts (HRAs) and non-HRA holding councils across the county. ¬†  ‚ÄčCHSCP has 3 distinct elements, which are summarised in more detail below.  ‚ÄčCouncil Housebuilding Support Service¬†  ‚ÄčMHCLG has funded the LGA to develop and administer a new Council Housebuilding Support Service. ¬†  ‚ÄčFollowing completion of a self-assessment exercise, the Service will provide councils with tailored access to training, online tools and resources to upskill their housebuilding teams against any identified gaps in their knowledge and/or skills base. ¬†  ‚ÄčThe Service will also complement the work of the Association of Directors of Housing by creating a peer-to-peer network to allow councils to work together more effectively to deliver social and affordable housing schemes.¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† ¬†  ‚ÄčDuring 2025-26, the Service aims to work with up to 75 councils.¬†  ‚ÄčFurther information about the Service can be found on the LGA‚Äôs website, including access to the self-assessment tool.¬†¬† ¬†  ‚ÄčFor clarity, councils receiving support through the Service should not also seek grant to address the same issues through the Council Housebuilding Support Fund (see below).  ‚ÄčAn expansion of the Pathways to Planning programme¬†  ‚ÄčBuilding on the success of the department‚Äôs Pathways to Planning programme, MHCLG has funded the LGA to create two new pathways for councils to recruit graduates to their housebuilding teams. At the same time, councils will be supported to enable these graduates to train for the necessary accreditation to become chartered surveyors or construction project managers.¬†  ‚ÄčThis follows engagement with stakeholders where these two professions were consistently highlighted as gaps in councils‚Äô workforces. These two new pathways aim to recruit up to 50 graduates in 2025-26, ready for onboarding with councils in September 2026-27. ¬†  ‚ÄčFurther information about the newly expanded Pathways to Planning programme can be found on the LGA‚Äôs website and we encourage councils interested in recruiting graduates to contact the LGA at the earliest possible opportunity.
